summary: P2 & P3 Features Testing Complete - All features working: Payment system (bank transfer + confirmation codes), Order tracking with status steps, Restaurant reviews/ratings, Backend refactoring with backward compat routes. Backend: 33/33 tests passed. Frontend: All UI flows verified. Fixed RestaurantMenu.jsx useParams bug.

issues: Search bar placeholder text could be more descriptive for accessibility

passed_tests: ✅ POST /api/payments/create - creates payment with confirmation code and bank info
✅ POST /api/payments/confirm - confirms payment with correct code
✅ GET /api/payments/bank-accounts - returns BAI, Multicaixa, Unitel Money details
✅ POST /api/reviews - creates restaurant review with rating 1-5
✅ GET /api/reviews/{restaurant_id} - gets reviews with average rating
✅ PATCH /api/orders/{order_id}/status - updates order status (confirmado, preparando, a_caminho, entregue)
✅ Frontend: Checkout page shows 3 payment methods (Transferência, Multicaixa, Unitel Money)
✅ Frontend: Checkout flow creates order -> shows bank details -> confirmation code -> payment confirmed
✅ Frontend: Orders page shows order cards with tracking steps visualization
✅ Frontend: Order tracking shows 4 steps (Confirmado, A Preparar, A Caminho, Entregue)
✅ Frontend: Restaurant menu page shows Reviews tab with star rating input
✅ Frontend: Restaurant page has search bar and cuisine filter pills
✅ Frontend: Payment success page shows 'Pagamento Confirmado!' with toast notification
seed_data_creation: Orders created via UI checkout flow, payments created and confirmed
context_for_next_testing_agent: P2 & P3 features fully tested. Payment flow: Order creation -> Bank transfer details (IBAN, NIF) -> Confirmation code (8 uppercase alphanumeric chars) -> Payment confirmed -> Order status updates. Order tracking shows 4 steps visualization. Reviews system allows 1-5 star ratings with comments. Backend routers refactored (tourism_router.py, properties_router.py) with backward compat routes in server.py. Fixed bug in RestaurantMenu.jsx where useParams was using wrong param name.

fixes_applied.issue: useParams() was using 'id' but route defined ':restaurantId'
fixes_applied.fix: Changed `const { id } = useParams()` to `const { restaurantId: id } = useParams()`

mocked_apis.note: Payment verification is SIMPLIFIED (code matching only, no real bank verification). Bank account details (IBAN, NIF) are EXAMPLE DATA, not real bank accounts.
